text.title: Agentic Workflow for Figma Plugin Ecosystem
text.summary: What agents can and cannot do with Figma plugins, prompt patterns, and workflow design.

list.item: Generate plugin configuration scripts and batch operation recipes for a human to run in Figma
Analyze Figma file exports (JSON via REST API) to identify opportunities for plugin automation
Produce plugin evaluation matrices scored against team criteria
Pre-generate Content Reel data files (JSON) that a human imports to the plugin
haiku — generating bulk operation recipes, plugin configuration checklists, content data files for Content Reel
sonnet — plugin ecosystem evaluation matrices, accessibility gap analysis from Figma JSON export, adoption policy drafts

section.title: Prompt and Template References
section.reference: Generate Content Reel-compatible JSON data file from name/email lists
Prompt for scored plugin adoption matrix against team criteria

antipattern.description: Agent proposes API-driven plugin automation endpoints for Magician, Automator, or Diagram.
antipattern.reason: These plugins have no server-accessible API. Figma plugin sandbox is browser-JS only. This is a persistent LLM hallucination — always verify against https://www.figma.com/plugin-docs/.

antipattern.description: Agent-generated Content Reel data using demographically homogeneous placeholder names.
antipattern.reason: Agent-generated names skew culturally; review for i18n/diversity before using in design files shown to stakeholders.

antipattern.description: Agent writes Figma plugin code based on LLM training data without verifying Plugin API version.
antipattern.reason: Figma Plugin API v2 has breaking changes; LLM-generated plugin code is often stale. Always validate against current https://www.figma.com/plugin-docs/.
